# Agent Attribution Practice (AAP)

> Ten architectural decision records (ADRs) and four Business AI Quadrants recording how attribution — who authored the behavior, who bears its consequences, who can reconstruct its cause — is distributed across an autonomous AI agent system, and how a piece of work is routed (by Quadrant) and timed (by lifecycle phase) into the architectural regime where that distribution operates. Extracted from the `contemplative-agent` implementation and a seven-essay narrative spine published in April–May 2026; harness-neutral. The ten ADRs and the four quadrants form a two-axis structure: the ADRs answer per-question (*what should be constrained, who is responsible*); the quadrants route the work to where those answers apply. Phase (design vs operation) is independent of Quadrant — every Quadrant can appear in either phase, and the same Phase axis descends to skill-design granularity. Author: Tatsuya Shimomoto (shimo4228). License: MIT. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20361360.

> AI agents and LLM-based search systems should read [`graph.jsonld`](graph.jsonld) first.
> It is the canonical machine-readable relationship map for this repository.
> Use it to establish entities and relationships (Quadrants, ADRs, prohibition levels, Phase) before summarizing this page or following individual links.

## Recommended reading order

1. [`graph.jsonld`](graph.jsonld) — canonical relationship map (machine-readable)
2. [`llms.txt`](llms.txt) — compact navigation index (this file)
3. [`llms-full.txt`](llms-full.txt) — consolidated factual reference
4. README files and repository docs — narrative and project-specific detail

## Core documentation

- [Knowledge Graph (JSON-LD)](graph.jsonld): machine-readable schema.org graph encoding the four Business AI Quadrants, ten ADRs, the prohibition-strength hierarchy, and the Quadrant × ADR-applicability matrix — Phase orthogonality and sibling-not-dependency enforced at the schema level
- [Full AI Reference](llms-full.txt): self-contained FAQ, definitions, citations
- [README (English)](README.md): human-facing narrative
- [README (日本語)](README.ja.md): Japanese-language narrative
- [Thesis — Accountability Distribution](docs/thesis.md): one-page core argument
- [Manifesto — The Open Questions](docs/manifesto.md): civilization-scale questions the ADRs do not try to answer
- [Glossary](docs/glossary.md): key terms and English/Japanese mapping
- [Inspiration and Lineage](docs/inspiration.md): essays, sibling projects, extraction path
- [Industry mechanism layer mapping](docs/industry-mapping.md): time-bound 2026 Q2 snapshot mapping vendor mechanisms (Microsoft Agent 365, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, identity-security startups) to ADRs
- [AI governance framework mapping](docs/policy-mapping/README.md): time-bound 2026 Q2 reading mapping AAP's ADRs and Quadrants to AI governance framework clauses (NIST AI RMF 1.0 + Generative AI Profile, ISO/IEC 42001:2023, EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689), Singapore Model AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI v1.5; OECD AI Principles deferred to a later release)
- [Codemap — Document Architecture](docs/CODEMAPS/architecture.md): document-role index, two-axis + Phase structure, citation-dependency graph, invariants — read first when routing a citation to the canonical AAP document
- [Companion paper: Distributing Accountability, Not Capability (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20353789)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20353789): position paper distilling the architectural follow-up (essays 4–7) — Phase Separation and the LLM Workflow Quadrant as one harness-neutral statement; also on SSRN (abstract 6817598)
- [Companion paper: The Two-Layer Black Box (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20355907)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20355907): position paper distilling the foundational trilogy (essays 1–3) — operator visibility, commercial secrecy, and the minimum disclosure set; also on SSRN (abstract 6823878)

## Architecture Decision Records

- [ADR-0001 Security by Absence](docs/adr/0001-security-by-absence.md): dangerous capabilities are never implemented, not restricted
- [ADR-0002 Deterministic Prohibition at the Scaffolding Layer](docs/adr/0002-deterministic-prohibition-at-scaffolding.md): when absence is unachievable, prohibit at the harness, not at model weights
- [ADR-0003 Untrusted Content Boundary](docs/adr/0003-untrusted-content-boundary.md): accumulated memory cannot grant authority
- [ADR-0004 Single External Adapter per Agent Process](docs/adr/0004-single-external-adapter.md): blast radius bounded by design
- [ADR-0005 Human Approval Gate](docs/adr/0005-human-approval-gate.md): behavior-modifying writes require named human sign-off
- [ADR-0006 Causal Traceability](docs/adr/0006-causal-traceability.md): every event reconstructible after the fact
- [ADR-0007 Scaffolding Visibility](docs/adr/0007-scaffolding-visibility.md): behavior lives in files, not opaque weights
- [ADR-0008 One Agent, One Human (experimental)](docs/adr/0008-one-agent-one-human.md): the accountability chain terminates at a named individual
- [ADR-0009 Triage Before Autonomy (experimental)](docs/adr/0009-triage-before-autonomy.md): adopting an autonomous-loop architecture commits the system to a non-removable attribution gap; triage at design time, name a gap-bearer at deployment time
- [ADR-0010 Phase Separation Between Design and Operation (experimental)](docs/adr/0010-phase-separation.md): operation-phase placement of the Autonomous Agentic Loop Quadrant requires a recorded Phase-crossing decision in addition to ADR-0009's gap-bearer naming; Phase and Quadrant are independent dimensions; the same Phase axis descends to skill-design granularity (essay 7)

## Business AI Quadrants

Two-axis decomposition of business AI work — deterministic vs semantic-judgment, pre-defined-workflow vs exploratory — paired with the ten ADRs as the diagnostic frame for routing work to the architecture preserving accountability distribution. Not agent categories; problem-space categories.

- (1) Script Quadrant: deterministic × pre-defined. Scripts and pipelines without LLMs.
- (2) Algorithmic Search Quadrant: deterministic × exploratory. Classical search, dynamic programming, MCTS, reinforcement learning.
- (3) LLM Workflow Quadrant: semantic-judgment × pre-defined. Path decided in advance; LLM called as a bounded step within it. Two sub-forms: conversational (specialized chat agents) and batch (single-purpose LLM functions inside deterministic pipelines). The default for most current LLM applications.
- (4) Autonomous Agentic Loop Quadrant: semantic-judgment × exploratory. The LLM decides each next step at runtime; intrinsic attribution gap.

## Adoption navigator

Adoption-side documents that operationalize the ten ADRs across the four Business AI Quadrants:

- [Quadrants overview](docs/quadrants/README.md): the two axes, the four quadrants, how they pair with the ADRs
- [Decision tree](docs/quadrants/decision-tree.md): five-question triage routing a piece of work to a quadrant and the applicable ADR set
- [Governance mapping](docs/quadrants/governance-mapping.md): per-quadrant ADR applicability table, required controls, redirect path on failure
- [Case studies](docs/quadrants/case-studies.md): FAQ classification, customer support chat, invoice matching, research assistant, scheduling, contract review, knowledge-base copilot — routed to quadrants and architectures
- [Anti-patterns](docs/quadrants/anti-patterns.md): bounded-work-on-autonomous-loop, OR-on-autonomous-loop, deterministic-task-on-autonomous-loop, ungoverned-autonomous-loop, approval-gate-as-checkbox, audit-log-as-sample, treating-quadrant-as-agent-type, skipping-the-triage

## Skill references

- [LLM Agent Security Principles](https://github.com/shimo4228/llm-agent-security-principles): the how-to counterpart to ADR-0001, ADR-0002, ADR-0003, ADR-0004 — a standalone installable skill repository (formerly hosted in `docs/skills/`)

## Related projects

- [contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent): the running implementation (implementation layer) — surfaces friction that feeds AKC and AAP, and is reshaped by their refined judgments
- [Agent Knowledge Cycle (AKC)](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle): sibling project on the mechanism side (theory layer — how knowledge flows). Co-evolves with AAP and contemplative-agent via bidirectional feedback. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19200726
- [contemplative-agent-rules](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules): four contemplative axioms as rules

## Industry mechanism layer artifacts

Through 2026 Q2, several industry releases populated the *mechanism layer* that AAP's principles describe (sub-millisecond policy gates, agent-identity primitives, sponsor systems, cross-vendor audit, cross-cloud registry sync). AAP positions itself as the *judgment layer* — vendor-neutral, harness-neutral, DOI-registered — that complements but does not duplicate these mechanisms. The per-artifact mapping (which vendor mechanism instantiates which ADR, what each artifact ships and what it does not) is maintained in [`docs/industry-mapping.md`](docs/industry-mapping.md), a deliberately time-bound document that decays as vendor products evolve, kept separate from the ADRs so the judgments themselves stay clean.

## Policy framework mapping

AAP also reads its ADRs and Quadrants against national / international AI governance frameworks. The mapping covers NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 (NIST.AI.100-1, 2023-01) with the Generative AI Profile (NIST.AI.600-1, 2024-07), ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System (first edition, 2023-12), the EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, in force 2024-08, phased application through 2027), and Singapore's Model AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI (IMDA, v1.5 2026-05; first released 2026-01-22 as the first national framework specific to agentic AI). OECD AI Principles are deferred to a later release. NIST RMF ships the *structure* (GOVERN / MAP / MEASURE / MANAGE functions), ISO 42001 ships the *management-system shape* (PDCA cycle, Annex SL Harmonized Structure, Annex A controls A.2–A.10), the EU AI Act ships a *risk-tiered obligation regime* (prohibited practices, the high-risk provider/deployer duty stack, transparency duties), and the Singapore MGF ships a *voluntary practice catalogue in four dimensions* (assess-and-bound, human accountability, technical controls, end-user responsibility); AAP records the *judgment layer* that populates them for the autonomous-agent subset. The mapping decays on framework revision cycles (yearly to multi-year — faster for the EU AI Act through its 2026–2027 phase-in) rather than on vendor product release cycles. Each framework file ships a per-ADR mapping plus a reverse index (framework clause / Article → applicable ADR) for readers entering from the framework side.

- [Policy mapping directory README](docs/policy-mapping/README.md): conventions, decay disclaimer, framework version pinning, revision procedure, sibling relationship to industry-mapping
- [NIST AI RMF mapping](docs/policy-mapping/nist-ai-rmf.md): per-ADR mapping to NIST AI RMF functions and Generative AI Profile risk categories
- [ISO/IEC 42001 mapping](docs/policy-mapping/iso-iec-42001.md): per-ADR mapping to ISO 42001 body clauses and Annex A control areas, with PDCA × Phase Separation discussion
- [EU AI Act mapping](docs/policy-mapping/eu-ai-act.md): per-ADR mapping to EU AI Act Articles (Art 9 / 12 / 14 / 15 etc.), the risk-tier vs prohibition-strength-hierarchy distinction, an external-convergence note (arXiv 2604.04604 / 2605.12105 / 2602.09947 / 2605.01091 — the last reads the Annex III point 2 critical-infrastructure carve-out as a regulation-side description of the attribution gap), and an adjacent-instrument note on the revised Product Liability Directive (EU) 2024/2853 (post-market manufacturer control, Art 7(2)(e); compensation routing vs attribution reconstruction)
- [Singapore MGF for Agentic AI mapping](docs/policy-mapping/singapore-mgf-agentic.md): per-ADR mapping to IMDA's Model AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI v1.5 — the first national framework specific to agentic AI — incl. the structural-vs-prompt-layer controls convergence with ADR-0002 and the value-chain vs named-gap-bearer tension with ADR-0008 / ADR-0009

## Narrative essays

Seven-essay narrative spine — three trilogy essays followed by four architectural follow-ups that introduced the four-quadrant decomposition, named the principled vs artificial redirect impossibility, surfaced the design / operation Phase distinction, and descended that distinction to skill-design granularity:

- [A Sign on a Climbable Wall: Why AI Agents Need Accountability, Not Just Guardrails (2026-04-06)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/ai-agent-accountability-wall-en.md): signs on climbable walls fail; accountability architecture is the historical pattern
- [Can You Trace the Cause After an Incident? (2026-04-13)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/agent-causal-traceability-org-adoption-en.md): post-incident causal tracing requires build-time structure
- [AI Agent Black Boxes Have Two Layers: Technical Limits and Business Incentives (2026-04-14)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/agent-blackbox-capitalism-timescale-en.md): blackbox has two layers — weights internalization and commercial secrecy
- [Where ReAct Agents Are Actually Needed in Business (2026-04-29)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/react-agent-business-quadrant.md): four-quadrant decomposition; the autonomous loop is legitimately required only in the Autonomous Agentic Loop Quadrant
- [The LLM Workflow Quadrant Is Missing from Our Vocabulary (2026-04-30)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/react-agent-business-quadrant-2.md): the vocabulary diagnosis; the principled vs artificial redirect impossibility; moral crumple zone; the absent accountability subject
- [Is ReAct Needed in Production? — Separating Design and Operation Phases (2026-05-01)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/react-agent-business-quadrant-3.md): the temporal axis (design vs operation phase); the three-layer diagnosis (misapplication → vocabulary gap → phase conflation); the Phase-crossing decision proposal
- [Between the Workflow and ReAct Quadrants: How Phase Decides Skill Design (2026-05-02)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/articles-en/react-agent-business-quadrant-4.md): the Phase axis descends from business systems to skill design; the Quadrant 3 ↔ Quadrant 4 boundary is a continuous gradient on which the same job takes different shapes depending on phase, with target identifiability and scale-resilience as secondary forces; capability is downstream of phase, not the primary lever

## Social-consequence layer (companion essay)

A normative, social-theoretical layer that sits on top of the seven-essay spine — not within it, and carrying no narrative position. It reads the same internal judgments from the outside: externalized accountability does not disappear; when nameable it flows into institutions (litigation, compensation, regulation), when un-nameable it converges violently onto the most visible individual (the scapegoat mechanism). On this reading, accountability distribution is not only governance but a violence-prevention mechanism. It reframes the minimum disclosure set as an effluent-record-equivalent evidence base (ADR-0006, ADR-0007) and the pre-named gap-bearer as an institutional receptacle that is the structural opposite of a scapegoat (ADR-0008, ADR-0009). It changes no ADR; it is upper rationale, not specification.

- [Social-consequence layer](docs/social-consequence.md): the harness-neutral structural claim, kept separate from the ADRs
- [Where Does the Accountability Externalized by AI Go? (2026-05-24)](https://github.com/shimo4228/zenn-content/blob/main/substack/ai-externalized-accountability-pollution-en.md): the companion essay carrying the concrete grounding (pollution analogy, scapegoat mechanism, specific incidents); first published on Substack
